items.headline: City Council reviews youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: The council received status updates on San José's Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including the Youth Empowerment Alliance and Safe Summer Initiative Grant programs. The council also reviewed the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan, signaling potential changes to library operations and facilities.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: An administrative zoning hearing scheduled for Wednesday, April 8 was cancelled and rescheduled to Wednesday, April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street that was on the April 8 agenda will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is set, meaning the project timeline has been pushed back.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project moves forward with consultant extension
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to extend the consultant agreement with DKS Associates for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project, indicating this infrastructure improvement is progressing toward implementation.

items.headline: 2026 street pavement replacement project contracts awarded
items.summary: The city council approved bids and awarded contracts for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project, which will address deteriorating street conditions across San José.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council received an update on Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, including edits to the plan and new scoring criteria for evaluating transportation projects. This plan will shape how Cupertino prioritizes biking, walking, and transit improvements over the coming years.

items.headline: Council considers changing vacancy process for appointed positions
items.summary: City Council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled and reduce related procedures. This could affect how quickly vacant council seats are filled and who gets a say in the process.

items.headline: 65-unit senior affordable housing complex approved downtown
items.summary: Trillium Senior Apartments will add 65 units of 100% affordable housing for seniors at 678 E St John St, representing significant affordable housing development in San José's core. The $8.8M project addresses the region's critical shortage of affordable senior living options.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition lawsuit against city proceeds in court
items.summary: The City Council met with legal counsel regarding ongoing litigation brought by the Garden Gate Coalition for Mary Avenue Safety against the City of Cupertino. This suggests an active dispute over street safety or development issues on Mary Avenue.
